original: Roy C. Sullivan of Virginia is struck by lightning for 7th time!
Roger Rabbit Cartoon Character debuts in "Who Framed Roger Rabbit?"
Peyo[Pierre Culliford], Belgiancartoonist(The Smurfs; Johan et Pirlouit), born in Brussels, Belgium (d. 1992)
First version ofAnne Frank's diary "Het Achterhuis" is published in the Netherlands
Science-fiction film "Blade Runner", directed byRidley Scott, starringHarrison Fordand Rutger Hauer, is released
First barbed wire patented by Lucien B. Smith of Ohio
Dunhuang manuscripts, including the Diamond Sutra, world's oldest surviving dated book, discovered by Daoist monk Wang Yuanlu in the Mogao Caves, China
VenetianElena Cornaro Piscopiais awarded a doctorate of philosophy from the University of Padua, the first woman to receive a university doctoral degree or PhD
First global satellite television program: "Our World" broadcast features 19 acts representing 19 nations including The Beatles singing "All You Need Is Love"
Progress M-34 Collides with and damages Mir Space Station
African National Congress leaderNelson Mandelameets with PresidentGeorge H. W. Bushat the White House
"Independence Day", directed by Roland Emmerich, and starringWill Smith,Bill PullmanandJeff Goldblumpremieres in Westwood, California
US PresidentHerbert Hooverauthorizes building of Boulder Dam (HooverDam)
Bill Moggridge [William], British designer (1st laptop computer), born in London, England (d. 2012)
Sonia Sotomayor, American judge (1st woman of color on the US Supreme Court 2009-), born in The Bronx, New York
Hermann Oberth, German physicist (founded modern astronautics), born in Hermannstadt, Austria-Hungary (d. 1989)
Paula Kahumbu, Kenyan-born wildlife activist (Hands Off Our Elephants campaign), born in Nairobi, Kenya
Bobby Bondshits a grand slam in his 1st major league game (San Francisco Giants)
Paris Themmen, American actor (Willy Wonka & the Chocolate Factory - Mike Teevee), born in Boston, Massachusetts

Warner Bros Records releases the sixth studio album by Prince, "Purple Rain"; his commercial peak, the film soundtrack album sells over 25 million copies, tops the charts in five countries, and wins two Grammy Awards and an Academy Award
Carly Simon, American Grammy and Academy Award-winning singer-songwriter ("Anticipation"; "You're So Vain"), born in New York City
Peter Blake, British visual artist who designed The Beatles Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band album cover, born in Dartford, Kent, England
The Beatles' single "Paperback Writer" goes #1 & stays #1 for 2 weeks
"A Tisket, A Tasket" byElla Fitzgeraldwith Chick Webb hits #1
Martina Navratilovawins her record 100th singles match at Wimbledon, defeating Elna Reinach of South Africa 4-6, 6-2, 6-4 in the first round
NBA Draft: Wake Forest power forwardTim Duncanfirst pick by San Antonio Spurs
Dikembe Mutombo, Congolese Basketball HOF center (8 x NBA All-Star; 4 × NBA Defensive Player of the Year; Atlanta Hawks, Denver Nuggets), born in Leopoldville, Congo (d. 2024)[1]
American Pancho González (41) beats Puerto Rican Charlie Pasarell (25) in 5 hour and 12 minute, 112-game marathon at Wimbledon
Future world heavyweight boxing championJoe Louismoves to 20-0 with 6th round KO of former championPrimo Carneraof Italy at Yankee Stadium, NYC
ICC Men's Cricket ODI World Cup, Lord's, London: India beats West Indies by 43 runs to win their first trophy; Player of Match: Mohinder Amarnath (IND) 3/12 from 7 overs
FIFA World Cup Final, El Monumental, Buenos Aires, Argentina: Striker Mario Kempes scores 2 goals as Argentina beats the Netherlands, 3-1 after extra time
FIFA World Cup: 1,500th goal in Cup'd history scored by Caceres of Argentina
MLB playerCal Ripken Jr. plays in his 1,000th consecutive game
Tatyana Averina, Russian speed skater 1K, 3K (Olympic gold 1976), born in Nizhny Novgorod, Russia (d. 2001)
Doug Gilmour, Canadian Hockey HOF center (Canada Cup gold 1987; Stanley Cup 1989 Calgary Flames; 2 x NHL All Star; Toronto Maple Leafs), born in Kingston, Ontario
Igor Stravinsky's ballet "The Firebird" for the Ballets Russes premieres at the Opéra de Paris, Paris
Kosmos 122, 1st Soviet weather satellite is launched
Galileo, 2nd Callisto Flyby (Orbit 9)
First passenger to fly commercially around the world in less than 100 hours
A lunar eclipse is the first astronomical event recorded in the American colonies
Previously unknown Winchester Manuscript of Sir Thomas Malory's "Le Morte d'Arthur" (1485) announced discovered in the library of Winchester College, England
David Douglas, Scottish botanist (Douglas fir), born in Scone, Perthshire, Scotland (d. 1834)[1]
Notre Dame du Haut chapel designed byLe Corbusieris consecrated in Ronchamp, France
US PresidentFranklin D. Rooseveltsigns Executive Order 8802, which forbids racial discrimination in the defense industry
Slovenia and Croatia declare independence from Yugoslavia
After a prolonged liberation struggle against Portuguese colonial rulers, Mozambique becomes independent as People Republic of Mozambique
Madagascar gains independence from France
Virginia becomes 10th state to ratify US constitution
WMCA radio in NYC plays The Beatles' "A Hard Days Night" album 10 days prior to its scheduled release date; record company moves release to June 26th
NHL approves franchises in Nashville, Atlanta, Columbus, and Minnesota-St Paul
Erki Nool, Estonian decathlete (Olympic Gold 2000), born in Võru, Estonia
Cub Augie Galan becomes 1st player to switch hit HRs in a game
Heavyweight boxerJoe LouisKOs Tami Mauriello
Joe LouisKOs Jersey Joe Walcott in 11 for heavyweight boxing title, at Yankee Stadium, NYC
Robert Reichel, Czech hockey forward (NY Islanders, Olympic gold 1998), born in Litvínov, Czech Republic
Dell Curry, American basketball guard and broadcaster (NBA Sixth Man of The Year 1994; Charlotte Hornets all-time points leader [9,839]), born in Harrisonburg, Virginia
David Paich, American rock keyboardist (Toto - "Rosanna"; "Africa"), born in Los Angeles, California

Tim Finn, New Zealand rock guitarist, singer, songwriter, and keyboardist (Split Enz - "I Got You"; Crowded House - "Don't Dream It's Over"), and theatre and film composer ("Puberty Blues"), born in Te Awamutu, New Zealand
Eddie Floyd, American R&B singer-songwriter (The Falcons, 1957-63; solo - "Knock on Wood", "Raise Your Hand"), born in Montgomery, Alabama
